page: Amazon Web Services r8a.metal-24xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server powered by AMD EPYC 9R45 processors. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying on external storage solutions and network-attached resources. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 40 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU workloads, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench evaluations. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers high throughput, achieving 521.03 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 215.78 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Cryptographic and vector execution capabilities are enhanced by hardware support for AVX-512, VAES, and SHA_NI instructions, which is reflected in top-tier Passmark CPU encryption and extended instruction test results.

The server demonstrates high efficiency for memory-intensive and concurrent processing tasks. It achieves 235.68 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and excels in database operations, software compilation, and static web serving. While it lacks GPU acceleration, the 96 dedicated EPYC cores deliver strong performance in CPU-based LLM inference, particularly in prompt processing and text generation for medium and large models. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise application hosting, and heavy data-processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.metal-24xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 96 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The server does not include local storage or GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET performance reaching 235.68 million operations per second. It is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, software compilation, and CPU-based large language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.metal-24xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 AMD EPYC cores and 768 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized platform featuring ninety-six dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-speed DDR5 RAM for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz
Top-tier memory bandwidth and CPU performance in benchmark testing
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage bundled
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and enterprise workloads
